---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Hayden Simm"
date: "2026-03-30"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

```{r}
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  drop_na(where(is.numeric))
```

PCA
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(ggfortify)
})

# Step 1: Select only relevant variables + grouping factors
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  select(
    COX_Activity,
    CS_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity,
    Assay_Temperature
  ) %>%
  # Remove rows with NA/Inf in the PCA variables ONLY
  filter(if_all(
    c(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  )) %>%
  mutate(
    group = interaction(
      Accliamtion_Temperature,
      Acclimation_Salinity,
      Assay_Temperature
    )
  )

# Step 2: Run PCA on selected variables
pca <- prcomp(
  killi_clean %>%
    select(COX_Activity, CS_Activity, LDH_Activity),
  scale. = TRUE
)

# Step 3: Plot PCA
autoplot(
  pca,
  data = killi_clean,
  colour = "Accliamtion_Temperature",
  shape  = "Acclimation_Salinity",
  frame  = TRUE,
  frame.type = "norm"
) +
  facet_wrap(~ Assay_Temperature) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of Metabolic Enzyme Activity & Condition",
    colour = "Acclimation Temp",
    shape  = "Acclimation Salinity"
  )

# Step 4: Outputs
summary(pca)
pca$rotation
```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)  # gives p-values
})

# Clean data
killi_clean <- killi %>%
  select(
    Fish_ID,
    CS_Activity,
    COX_Activity,
    LDH_Activity,
    Assay_Temperature,
    Accliamtion_Temperature,
    Acclimation_Salinity
  ) %>%
  filter(if_all(
    c(CS_Activity, COX_Activity, LDH_Activity),
    ~ is.finite(.)
  ))

# CS model
mod_CS <- lmer(
  CS_Activity ~ Assay_Temperature *
    Accliamtion_Temperature *
    Acclimation_Salinity +
    (1 | Fish_ID),
  data = killi_clean
)

# COX model
mod_COX <- lmer(
  COX_Activity ~ Assay_Temperature *
    Accliamtion_Temperature *
    Acclimation_Salinity +
    (1 | Fish_ID),
  data = killi_clean
)

# LDH model
mod_LDH <- lmer(
  LDH_Activity ~ Assay_Temperature *
    Accliamtion_Temperature *
    Acclimation_Salinity +
    (1 | Fish_ID),
  data = killi_clean
)

# Summaries (with p-values)
summary(mod_CS)
summary(mod_COX)
summary(mod_LDH)
```
